behavior_summary: U8P_EYE::EYE::look is a short process-handler invoked when an actor 'looks' at or inspects the EYE object. It configures per-process state (sets process info and a process-exclude flag), causes the target actor/process to emit the literal message "seeker ", and then suspends.

The handler constructs a temporary string pointer for "seeker ", calls Item::bark on the supplied process/actor pointer, frees the string, captures Item::bark's return value, runs an 'implies' style operation with that return value, consumes the result into a small temporary, and then suspends the script.
All state changes observed are local to the involved process (per-process info and process-exclude); no global game flags are read or written by this script.
notable_control_flow: Entry (Event 0 / U8P_EYE::look): set per-process info flag, set process-exclude flag on the relevant process.
Build/convert literal string "seeker " into a pointer, push the target process pointer ([BP+06h]) and the string, then call Item::bark.
After returning from Item::bark, free the temporary string, capture the return value, apply an 'implies' operation with that return, pop result into a temp, then suspend the process.
open_questions: Which exact per-process info field (numeric id) is being set by the 'set info' opcode here? The disassembly shows the opcode but the concrete info id is not explicit.

What semantic effects does the 'implies' operation have after Item::bark in this engine (e.g., conditional continuation, signal, or side-effect)? The script pops the result and then suspends, so its practical effect is unclear.
Is [BP+06h] always the player PID in all call sites, or can other processes trigger EYE::look using different PIDs? This is inferred but not proven by this snippet alone.

main_function.name: U8P_EYE::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to an examine/U8P_EYE::look event by setting per-process state for the EYE and causing the target process to 'bark' the text "seeker ", then handle the bark return and suspend.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — U8P_EYE::look/examine trigger (invoked when an actor/player inspects or 'looks at' the EYE).

function_map.name: U8P_EYE::look
function_map.role: Handle the object's U8P_EYE::look/examine event: set per-process flags, call Item::bark with "seeker ", process return value, then suspend.

external_calls.purpose: Make the target process/actor emit or display the short literal message "seeker ". Item::bark is called with the target process pointer (inferred at [BP+06h]) and the string pointer.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_EYE::EYE::look is a short process-handler invoked when an actor 'looks' at or inspects the EYE object. It configures per-process state (sets process info and a process-exclude flag), causes the target actor/process to emit the literal message "seeker ", and then suspends.
